message:
fix(doctor): distinguish invalid/missing/unreadable JSONL from valid-with-count

The doctor report previously collapsed "JSONL exists and has N valid
records" and "JSONL exists but parsing failed" into the same
`Option<usize>` return type, using `None` for both the JSONL-missing
case and the JSONL-parse-failed case. That meant the `counts.db_vs_jsonl`
check emitted an identical "JSONL not found; cannot compare counts"
warning for a genuinely missing JSONL and for a parse-error JSONL, which
is misleading when the operator has an existing-but-corrupt JSONL on
disk.

Introduce an explicit `JsonlCountState` enum with four variants:
- `Available(usize)` — parsed successfully, N valid records
- `Invalid`         — file exists, `validate_jsonl_issue_records`
                      reported ≥1 invalid line
- `Missing`         — no JSONL file found in the beads dir
- `Unreadable`      — JSONL existed but the read errored before
                      validation could run

and thread it through `check_jsonl`, `inspect_doctor_jsonl`,
`inspect_doctor_database`, `inspect_existing_doctor_database`, and
`check_db_count`. Each state now produces its own `counts.db_vs_jsonl`
message ("JSONL is invalid; cannot compare counts", "JSONL not found;
cannot compare counts", "JSONL unreadable; cannot compare counts") so
an operator can tell the difference between "no file" and "unparseable
file" without having to cross-reference the `jsonl.parse` check.

Tests:
- updated existing jsonl.parse regressions to assert the new
  `JsonlCountState::Invalid` return value
- new `test_check_jsonl_returns_count_only_for_valid_records`
- new `test_collect_doctor_report_skips_count_comparison_for_invalid_jsonl`
  which builds a DB with one issue, writes a JSONL with one valid row
  plus one `{bad json}` row, and asserts the counts check emits the new
  "JSONL is invalid" message rather than a false-positive divergence
  warning.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
